# Compute Spot Index

URL: https://chiragmirani.github.io/compute-spot-index/

Compute Spot Index is a public AI compute price tracker. It summarizes public Vast.ai rental offers into median prices per graphics processor-hour, then translates those prices into rough annual value after operating costs and estimated present value for a 100,000-processor AI data center.

Primary data:
- Public Vast.ai rental offers, normalized to price per graphics processor-hour.
- Saved public snapshot: https://chiragmirani.github.io/compute-spot-index/data/latest-public.json

Core formula:
- Annual value after operating costs = median hourly rental price * 100,000 graphics processors * 8,760 hours * 65% utilization - estimated annual operating costs.
- Estimated annual operating costs use Epoch AI's $0.9 billion annual operating expense estimate for a one-gigawatt AI data center, scaled to a 120-megawatt proxy site: about $108 million per year.
- Estimated present value after operating costs uses 10 years and a 10% to 15% discount rate.

Reference context:
- Epoch AI, "Frontier Data Centers". Published online at epoch.ai. Retrieved from "https://epoch.ai/data/data-centers" [online resource].
- Epoch AI, "We estimate the cost breakdown for 60% of the total cost of ownership of a one-gigawatt AI data center". Published online at epoch.ai. Retrieved from "https://epoch.ai/data-insights/ai-datacenter-cost-breakdown" [online resource].

Caveat:
- These are public spot-market prices, not hyperscaler procurement costs or net operating margins. The estimates use a broad operating-cost assumption rather than site-specific contracts and still exclude financing structure, downtime, hardware replacement timing, and the risk that rental prices fall as supply expands.
